Ladies and gentlemen, welcome to phase number three video one, different services for different agencies underneath our products and services category. Now, within this particular video, we're going to go through it a lot. At one point, I'm going to list off the services underneath the different packages that at one point I offered as a digital agency for customizing every package and doing 15k minimums as a personality brand development agency. So I'm going to give you the exact things, literally, in a downloadable format and I'm going to list them off into video verbally. So you guys can see I'm also show individually the name of each one of these things. So feel free to get a pen and paper out, feel free to copy these to the T inside of your digital agency. When you list them off as services, just make sure that you have the ability to actually fulfill the services in which you choose to represent. Don't just add all of them as a value. I could do that because I could do that stuff. You have to have the right team. You have to be able to deliver. A lot of you guys might come from Grant Cardone, which gives the mindset that you need to do things first and then figure the rest out later. And that's cool in certain instances and scenarios, such as investing into yourself or such as trying a new type of food or something like that. You place an order and then you try the food out later, right? Figure it out. That can also go for different things with finances and opportunity, but sometimes I need to understand that you don't want to sell people things that you can't deliver. It's one thing that you don't want to do. Meaning have the confidence once again to go into somebody's business for your first sale and sell the shit out of them as if you sold it a thousand times. But that comes from confidence, not from you telling the person you can execute something without making them aware that you don't have the experience to execute it and that you're going to be trying to do it. What I'm trying to save you from is a critical lesson that I learned in business the hard way, selling any product and service, which is don't oversell and then under deliver. You have to understand that a satisfied customer is one step away from being an unsatisfied customer. You want to over deliver for everybody. There's different degrees of exchange. You want to always be in the abundance exchange. You don't want to be in the satisfaction exchange or at the third degree, which is the unsatisfied exchange because that, ladies and gentlemen, is going to get you a lot of refunds in business, which you also don't. So when we start talking about products and services, I'm going to list off some of the different types of agencies and what services you might offer. So one of the one of the absolute greatest things I ever learned in business was systems and processes. People absolutely love systems and processes. And if you don't have the ability to walk people through a fluent process from A to Z, meaning they sign up with you, or you just can't get communication with them, let's go all the way back to the point where you shake their hand. They want to feel like they're in a process from the point they actually meet you, all the way to the point they're satisfied client and an old delivered client inside your business. So in my business, I'll show you this guy's strategize, design, optimize, develop, deploy, and analyze. That was our six step process at the time. We call it the battle plan blueprint, the BPD. You can also download these below. You can also just press the button below. There's something that says social services or something like that, whatever the link's named. Click on it, it'll download this sheet. But here's the thing. I'm going to explain to you how it's laid out first. When you have a price sheet, I had a lot of mentors talking about this thing. It's called a VFP. VFP stands for valuable final product. And a valuable final product. Something really important to your business because without it, you're not really delivering a product. Nobody in their right mind is just going to sign up for a service and not expect something from it. You're not going to sign up for this course and not expect the result. That's what a product is. A product is another fancy word for a result. A VFP is a fancy way of saying it's a product or a result that you're going to give them that over delivers. So this is how I lay out and how you're going to lay out a VFP, a valuable final product. So the package name and the actual product needs to have a title. So this is called the social media package. We ended up nicknaming it my company's social services. It sounded a little better and it was SS for short. So it was cool for us. Anyway, didn't have what the product is. I'm going to read this off to you guys. You can see with a well thought out reverse engineered strategy, we help determine what social media channels your brand should be using ways to create audience engagement and awareness and establish measurable goals and benchmarks to track your social media market in progress. We then execute content creation and distribution and are always moving towards establishing benchmarks and goals. Put that in your digital playbook. Feel free. Then you have this right here. You have the bullet points of what's included on the bullet points here. What you want to list off are the actual line items in a sense like if, for example, you go to a restaurant, you see a line item breakdown of everything on their menu with a price next to it. I don't list off individual prices next to these things because I don't do all the card services. This is another way that you can sell things. If you'd like, you don't have to sell the whole package. Let's say you're more of a piecemeal person or you're more of like a scraps kind of individual and you just want to sell stuff that's cool. Social media strategy development. So this is what's included when you spent $4,000 a month or the beginning of my business $1,800 a month. Keep in mind, the only difference between two prices was the mindset of my perceived value. So if you purposely want to go out of your way to sell $4,000 to start, I'd recommend it because there's no difference between $1,800 and $4,000. It's only the type of customer you'll attract and you'll find people at $4,000. They want a result. Absolutely. They want to have some kind of ROI since it's enough money, but I'm going to show you how to sell it so they don't require an ROI and you can sell for a higher price when we talk about that in the sales portfolio. You can do the same thing with services, you could strip them away and you could change up the prices. I personally, I just found it was easier to tell everybody that I had a mini-movement business. Now, I got this mentorship from Dan Fleschman and Brandon Hampton. Dan Fleschman is the youngest founder of a publicly traded company in history. And Brandon Hampton is the number one social media influence in the world ranked by Forbes. He's the admin on the Kardashian accounts. They're the ones that are brokering deals between brands and the Kardashians doing 250k a pop for a post. They're the ones that are angel investors to over 40 something companies each. And they are direct mentors of mine whenever I fly out to LA. If you guys follow me on Snapchat at the Life of Pains or if you follow me on Instastories at Jeremy, you guys will see all the time that I'm at some funky workout living office in LA or Hollywood, wherever I'm at, right? That office is Brandon and Dan's offices. And that's where all the influencers come and shoot content for their channels. Now, Brandon and Dan at one point taught me when I was selling these types of services that I needed to have what's called a minimum. So in this case on this on this package right here, we'll see I have a $12,000 minimum. Now, if you're a digital agency, these are digital agency services. Then they're things that you offer people when you want to do more for them than just social media marketing. Now, some common services that I'll list off that you may understand before I list off all these technical ones. Let's leave them on this a line item list. So you sell people with words they understand, and then you give them value by listing off all of the technical shit that you would never use words with because you'd overwhelm people. You want them to be overwhelmed when they're looking at the value that they're going to get with the price next to it. When you're selling them, you want to only mention price once and then you want to build all the value around words that they understand, bring it into their reality and then like I said overwhelm them with value and price sheet. So words that you'll understand that you offer as a digital agency for services. Here we go. Landing page creation, web development, funnel creation, funnel optimization, business consulting, web consulting, social consulting, you can do email marketing, SMS marketing, voicemail marketing, marketing automation, you can do marketing automation development, you can do CRM database management, you could do CRM optimization because not using fusion software, those fancy tools like active campaign or Salesforce or par die or mail chamber, constant contact or A-web or whatever right. You got to be willing to upsell those things in individual services.
